通过基准测试（除非他们玩过它们），似乎处于 Opus 4.7 水平左右，这就是 Elon 在 https://x.com/elonmusk/status/2074911038286295049。

我想光标数据非常有用。

https://docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-4.5

comments.textZhHtml: 此外，缓存命中定价是输入定价的 25%（2 美元 vs 0.50 美元）。长代理工作流程由缓存输入主导。美国前沿实验室通常以输入价格的 10% 进行此操作，而 DeepSeek/小米等将其达到极端的 1% 范围（这就是为什么这些在现实世界的代理循环中运行成本低廉，每次运行数十个工具调用）

comments.author: HarHarVeryFunny
comments.textZhHtml:
只是为了添加上下文（没有双关语），OpenAI 还根据上下文使用情况收取不同的费用，GPT 5.5 低于 200K 为 5/30 美元，高于 10/45 美元。

无论上下文使用情况如何，Anthropic 都有固定价格。

这些按代币定价方案不能直接比较，因为这些模型都使用不同数量的代币，即使对于输入也是如此（Anthropic 最近的标记生成器更改为完全相同的输入生成了 30% 的标记）以及推理，并且上下文/标记的使用也因 Claude Code 使用 3 倍于 Pi 的上下文/标记而变化很大。

comments.author: Sidio
comments.textZhHtml:
我不知道，谢谢。

有谁知道为什么他们会对更高的上下文使用收取更多费用（除了他们可以收取的费用之外）？

comments.author: staticshock
comments.textZhHtml:
将整个上下文（对话的完整线程、所有工具调用输出等）视为一次性提交给 LLM 的一条消息，以便它可以生成下一个标记（这只是下一个单词甚至音节。）生成该标记后，它会附加到上下文，并且整个上下文再次用于生成下一个标记。继续这样做，直到生成整个响应。上下文越大，模型在生成每个下一个标记时需要注意的内容就越多。

打个比方：想象你的朋友是一本未完成的书的作者。他们死时写下了 19 章，临终时要求你写第 20 章。假设您能够胜任这项任务，那么只有花时间吸收到目前为止所写的全部内容，您才能做好这项工作。

这就是法学硕士的工作方式。上下文缓存是对此的优化，但它有其局限性。

comments.author: HarHarVeryFunny

AI 服务成本显然主要是硬件折旧而不是运营成本（电力等），如果你的大型上下文请求占用 VRAM 一小部分时间，那么你就要为当时发生的折旧付费！

e. H100 的购买成本为 20-40,000 美元，使用寿命可能为 3 年，如果这 3 年 24x7 运行，仅消耗大约 2000 美元的电力。

stories.title: Decoding the obfuscated bash script on a Uniqlo t-shirt
stories.titleZh: 解读优衣库T恤上的混淆bash脚本
stories.author: speerer
summaryZh: When my wife said to me “Let me show you a t-shirt I saw…”, I wasn’t sure what to expect, but it definitely wasn’t an obfuscated bash script printed on the back designed to print a happy Easter egg message. I’m not in the habit of clickbaity headlinesI’ve no idea at all how many views this site gets, but I’m willing to bet it’s not even double-digit humans per month. but I can see why subeditors have such fun with them. The title above is, strictly speaking, entirely accurate, but probably not what you think. The obfuscated code in question is actually an easter egg, it’s being supplied via Uniqlo stores on an excellent t-shirt designed by Akamai in support of their Peace for All campaign. And it’s very cool! The front has a heart in curly braces:While the back has a big alphanumeric block: Is that … a shebang?!My wife was right that I’d want to see it. Was that… a shebang? Take a closer look at the text block: Yes, a shebang! On a t-shirt sold in a high street store, no less. And it is clearly a base64 encoded Here string being fed to eval via base64 --decode. Interesting. I told my wife "that’s basically how people ship viruses’ and bought it. OCR was cumbersomeThere was good news and bad news: The bad news was that base64 hasn’t got error correction, meaning that the transcription would need to be perfect. Sigh. The good news was that the string seemed to be intact - at least, it terminated with the expected padding and had matching quotes and braces. This is a good thing because Uniqlo x Akamai sells another design of shirt in the same range which is plainly incompleteFor example, its imports are truncated and it ends “retu” instead of “return”. This is a pity, because it’s a really nice colour combination and contains the highly idiomatic instruction go doStuff(msg, work... which anyone can relate to., a truncated crop from a wider text block which could never compile. I ran OCR in a few ways: First, using the built-in OCR of the circle-to-search feature on Android, which is often very good. Second, by using Tesseract with a few options and tweaks. And third by running it through Claude. After diffing the three to look for mismatches and getting Claude to output a table of locations for quick scanning, it became trivial but time-consuimg to tidy up the remainder. The resulting string was: 
The decoded scriptAfter Base64 decoding, the resulting script is a welcoming and nicely commented Easter egg:#!/bin/bash

stories.title: Microsoft fire idTech team at Id software
stories.titleZh: 微软解雇了 Id software 的 idTech 团队
stories.author: bauc
summaryZh: id Software are without a doubt one of the most important game developers in the first person genre history. The technology empowering their games, idTech powers a shocking number of games and even game engines ( we ranked it 4th in our Most Important Game Engines of all Time ranking). This era might be coming to a close as it’s being reported that most, if not all, developers working on idTech at id Software have been fired.

This comes as part of the MASSIVE layoffs across all Xbox divisions. Asha Sharma, the new CEO of Xbox tweeted the email that was sent to the entire company. It explains why the layoffs are happening, starting with:

comments.author: ndiddy
comments.textZhHtml: 我认为随着时间的推移，我们会看到类似的事情继续发生。作为一家游戏公司，拥有自己的引擎意味着您必须能够培养工具方面的内部专业知识。您的员工会知道这一点，并且可能会做坏事，例如索要更多钱，因为他们知道更换他们会严重损害生产力。同时，解雇整个引擎团队并切换到 UE5 意味着您可以接触到大量了解 UE5 的低薪承包商。当你开始一个游戏项目时，你可以雇佣一群人，然后在项目完成后将他们全部解雇，并根据需要进行冲洗和重复。它可以让您将您的员工视为一种可替换的商品，可以根据金钱的需要而扩大或缩小规模，而不是一个有凝聚力的熟练工匠团队。

comments.author: munificent
comments.textZhHtml:
所有这些都是事实，并且在游戏行业几十年来一直如此。

这个跷跷板的另一面是：游戏从根本上来说是一种新奇行业。玩家喜欢一定程度的熟悉感，但他们想要新的体验。每个游戏引擎都有一种“颗粒”，它往往会产生具有特定外观和感觉的游戏。 Unity 的扁平化着色和浮动物理就是一个特别明显的例子。因此，如果您试图制作一款不符合这种要求并为玩家提供不同东西的游戏，那么使用广泛使用的游戏引擎可能会让您处于不利地位。

随着越来越多的工作室整合到同一个引擎上，越来越多的玩家会厌倦这种千篇一律的做法，并向其他工作室提供更多奖励。随着越来越多的工作室做自己的事情，玩家将被新奇事物所饱和，而不使用引擎的好处将会下降。不存在稳定平衡。

comments.author: Cpoll
comments.textZhHtml:
> 每个游戏引擎都有一种“颗粒”，它往往会产生具有特定外观和感觉的游戏。

我认为这有点神话。虚幻经常受到这种批评，但这通常是因为许多工作室选择坚持渲染默认值，这确实导致了某种外观。

就这一点而言，将虚幻或 Unity 配置为独特的“颗粒”可能比开发自己的引擎便宜得多。还可以使用自定义物理场而不是引擎中内置的物理场。

comments.author: willis936
comments.textZhHtml: 我可以告诉你，id tech 6 引擎游戏看起来和它们一样好，并且输出 200+ fps，这在某种程度上是相当新颖的，大型史诗游戏和他们的旗舰 UE5（人们可能不会想到任何精心优化的案例研究）甚至在十年后都接近匹配。  包揽整个行业的回报是什么？  提前退休。

comments.author: ahartmetz
comments.textZhHtml: 《毁灭战士》和《毁灭战士：永恒》的性能和流畅性也给我留下了深刻的印象。即使从 HDD（在由剩余部件制成的计算机上）播放时，当高分辨率纹理和几何图形加载速度不够快时，引擎也只会显示低分辨率版本，然后平滑地混合到更高的分辨率，而不会出现卡顿。即使在比大多数其他游戏所需的 GPU 低 2-3 层的情况下，这些游戏的运行效果也能如此出色，这简直就是一个小奇迹。

comments.author: midnightclubbed
comments.textZhHtml:
id tech 可能是最后一个使用签名游戏向授权商展示引擎的引擎。 idTech、Unreal Engine 和 Crytech 的商业模式曾经是“制作外观精美的游戏，然后将引擎出售给授权商”。 该模型的问题在于，无论“外观令人惊叹”的游戏是什么，您都会获得游戏代码的一部分，并被告知要弄清楚它。 如果你想制作《战争机器》的克隆版，那么虚幻引擎 3 是完美的...对于你必须重写和实现大量代码的其他游戏类型。

虚幻引擎成功地发展和扩展了引擎，使其在某种程度上与游戏无关，crytech 成为亚马逊旗下的木材场并消亡，idTech 成为专门用于 iD 游戏的引擎（即与所有其他游戏或发行商自定义引擎相同的商业模式）。

iD tech 的渲染管道是专注于 iD 当年发布的一款 AA 游戏，并让这款游戏在中高端 PC 和当前游戏机上高效、美观地运行。 UE5 专注于为任何人提供工具（相对）轻松地制作可发布到各种设备的精美游戏和应用程序。

comments.author: danbolt
comments.textZhHtml:
我认为他们获得如此好的帧率的一个重要原因是他们对着色器计数的保守态度。一位工作人员在接受 DigitalFoundry 的《毁灭战士：黑暗时代》采访时提到了这一点。我想知道他们的内部技术是否使这种限制变得更容易。

与其他引擎相比，制造新的物质资产对于技术水平较低的人来说很容易随心所欲地做。

comments.author: midnightclubbed

stories.title: Why skilled workers come to Germany and then leave again
stories.titleZh: 为什么技术工人来到德国后又会再次离开
stories.author: theanonymousone
summaryZh: "Anyone who wants to shape immigration successfully must also understand emigration," Laura Gossner, a researcher in migration and international labor studies with the Institute for Employment Research (IAB), said at a press conference in Berlin.

The IAB conducted an online survey of people aged 18 to 65 who had immigrated to Germany before April 2025 and then chose not to stay, asking them what factors influenced their decision to leave the country again.

comments.author: ikut3hva
comments.textZhHtml:
大家好

我通常不会评论这样的话题，因为其中涉及很多偏见和不同的观点。最后，我相信只有真正经历过的人才能真正理解；否则，它往往会成为另一个判断。

我们是一个年收入超过20万欧元的东盟家庭（很抱歉提到TC，但这是有原因的——请在判断之前继续阅读）。我们在这里住了六年多了，你知道吗？我仍然没有获得永久居留权或德国公民身份，只是因为我没有 B1 证书。因此，首先要做的事情是：无论您为国家做出了多少贡献，如果您想获得居留权并在这里稳定生活，德语都是必须的。

当官员告诉我我没有资格获得永久居留权时，我真的感到很沮丧。也是在那一刻，我开始觉得也许我实际上并不需要在这个国家永久居留。

故事 2：在国际工作环境中，德语在 IC 层面上可能并不重要。但我见过无数次这样的情况：德国人互相交换了一个眼神，突然间最终的决定并不是会议上达成的一致。随着时间的推移，我了解到幕后有许多不成文的规则，当你说他们的语言时，你开始理解它们。

一件好事是，也许我们仍然很幸运。我们在没有完全了解法律、政府制度或税收规则的情况下购买了第一套房子。我们只是努力工作，并以我们认为从长远来看可持续的方式玩游戏。无论发生什么，我们知道我们还有很多其他地方可以去。

我们的孩子的母语是德语，但他们也愿意在家里多花点力气说我们的母语。

如果你问我对德国的移民和移民有什么建议，我会说：生命短暂——赤身裸体！

comments.author: dgs_sgd
comments.textZhHtml:
我很欣赏你的观点，但我很好奇 B1 熟练程度实际上意味着什么，这就是我发现的 [1]：

- 理解关于工作、学校或休闲等熟悉主题的清晰标准演讲的要点 - 管理在德语地区旅行时发生的大多数情况 - 就熟悉的主题生成简单、相互关联的文本 - 描述经历、事件、梦想、希望和抱负，并简要解释您的意见或计划

对于那些想要将使用该语言的县作为永久家园的人来说，这似乎是一个合理的母语熟练程度标准。

[1] https://www.sprachenatelier-berlin.de/en/topic/3736.german-p...

comments.author: TheOtherHobbes
comments.textZhHtml:
B1 是一个完全公平的最低标准。对于许多国家来说，期望居民具备基本的会话能力是很正常的。

这也是政府对居住信息提出的明确要求。所以这并不奇怪。

comments.author: dgs_sgd
comments.textZhHtml: 就像，我想象如果我搬到一个国家并且无法用他们的语言做 B1 的事情，我每天做基本生活的事情都会遇到障碍。

comments.author: 14u2c
comments.textZhHtml:
西欧和中欧的大部分地区没有。每个人的英语都说得很好。

这实际上可能是一个问题，因为大多数人一意识到您不是母语者就改用英语，这会让学习变得更加困难。

comments.author: marcus_holmes
comments.textZhHtml:
这是我的经历。我在柏林住了将近四年，一直在努力学习德语（我达到了 A2，这并不多）。但实际上在柏林获得德语的经验/沉浸式学习是很困难的 - 每个 50 岁以下的人都会说一口流利的英语，当我说出第一句话时，他们就转向英语。

我在那里时曾为两家德国公司工作过，两家公司的整个技术团队都用英语做所有事情。这在那里很正常。在其中一篇文章中，（德国）首席执行官用英语向整个公司（约 1000 名员工）发表了市政厅演讲。

所以，是的，德国政府说“你必须至少达到 B1 才能住在这里”是可以理解的，但这并没有认识到一个事实，即完全没有德语也完全有可能在柏林生活和工作。

显然，这在其他德国城市是不同的。如果我们搬到慕尼黑或科隆，我会面临更大的学习德语的压力，也会有更多的接触德语和更多的沉浸感，这将有助于学习德语。

comments.author: thayne
comments.textZhHtml:
即使当我在拜仁（巴伐利亚）度假时，很多人在意识到我的德语不太好时也改用英语。这很令人沮丧，因为我想练习与母语人士交谈。

尽管如此，也有人只说德语。

comments.author: doikor
comments.textZhHtml: 如果你想学习，你就假装你不会说英语，然后继续说德语。他们会很快切换回来。

comments.author: flakeoil
comments.textZhHtml:
这是正确的方法。说您想要的语言。即使他们用英语回复您，并且他们知道您会说英语，也请继续用德语。他们会理解的。他们改用英语是因为他们想提供帮助。如果您继续使用德语，他们会理解您更喜欢这种方式。

另一件事是，一旦您开始用一种语言与某人（您经常遇到的人）交谈，以后就很难切换到另一种语言。所以如果你从英语开始，就很难再回到德语。但如果你从德语开始，即使你不太流利，你也会很自然地和那个人说德语。

comments.author: throwaway2037
comments.textZhHtml:
您是否询问过是否可以搬到其他城市并远程工作？ 即使只有 1-2 年，也可能确实有助于提高您的德语。 搬到德累斯顿、汉诺威或汉堡会有帮助。 （德国人可能会推荐一些距离柏林 1-2 小时火车/汽车车程的小城市，这些城市会使用更多德语！）您可以每周来办公室一次。

另一个提示：始终用德语开始对话。 如果对方改用英语，请礼貌地请他们说德语，并解释说您正在学习德语。 50 年前，我的父母搬到荷兰时也做了类似的事情。 当地的店主很想和我的父母一起练习英语，但我的父母也很想练习荷兰语。

comments.author: marcus_holmes
comments.textZhHtml:
这确实是一个很好的建议，但我发现很难付诸实践，因为我的德语太糟糕了。德语不是一种你可以敲出一个结构错误的句子的语言；词位不如词形变化重要，所以如果词形变化错误，听者就毫无意义。我确实尝试过几次，但当每个人意识到他们的英语比我的德语好得多时，他们都对语言的痛苦屠宰失去了耐心。

我本来会搬到城市，但是a：新冠病毒b：我妻子在那里也有一份工作c：我真的很喜欢柏林，真的很喜欢在那里生活

comments.author: throwaway2037
comments.textZhHtml: 我喜欢这种努力！ 作为一个成年人，学习一门新语言就已经成功了一半。 真正帮助我的是：创建您的自己的自定义 Anki 牌组。 顾名思义，就是从零开始。 每次你尝试失败（或者想到一些你不知道怎么说的事情）时，用英语/蹩脚的德语给自己写一张纸条，写上失败的句子/想法。 在家里，使用翻译工具（法学硕士/谷歌/其他）来构建正确的德语句子。 接下来，将其添加到您的 Anki 套牌中。 就我个人而言，我只添加完整的句子，而不仅仅是词汇，因为它添加了更自然的上下文。 仅添加您将使用的真实内容。 第一天，您将拥有一套包含一张卡片的 Anki 套牌。 如果您确实有动力，请尝试每天添加一张卡，但每周只需添加 1-2 张卡即可快速改善牌组。 由于甲板实在是太小了，乘坐 S-Bahn/U-Bahn 火车只需五分钟就可以穿过它。 而且，每一句话对你都有用。 额外奖励：向以德语为母语的人询问您的 Anki 甲板句子。 他们可能会很乐意与您谈论德语，因为您会表现出您的承诺。

comments.author: bryanrasmussen
comments.textZhHtml:
>每个 50 岁以下的人都会说一口流利的英语，当我说出第一句话时，我就开始说英语了。

好吧，也许近年来情况已经发生了变化。当我来柏林探望几天的家人或开车去参加会议时，我总是很恼火，不可避免地会有这样的时候，我必须向某人寻求帮助，我必须用英语询问，因为我不会说德语（尽管由于年轻的沉浸感，几周后我又开始记住它），他们不可避免地会用德语回答。

当我们在公园停下来询问会议中心在哪里时，旁边只有几个人，我可以理解。路，但在火车站询问售票员我应该走哪条路线才能到达特定车站并得到德语答复似乎特别粗鲁。

无论如何，尽管德国人在帮助方面有一些良好的经验，但我还是有过太多负面的经历。

comments.author: marcus_holmes
comments.textZhHtml:
即使在德国，柏林人也因直率到粗鲁而闻名。

我们在英语圈中认为粗鲁的事情，在他们看来却是高效的。一开始可能会非常令人不快，但你确实会习惯它。

comments.author: bryanrasmussen
comments.textZhHtml: 在大多数其他以高效着称的国家，用提问者显然不会说的语言回答不会被认为是高效的。

comments.author: atoav
comments.textZhHtml:
唱个反调：这对他们来说确实很高效——

说德国人人都会说英语，多少有些言过其实。诚然，在学术或商业环境中，或是某些社交圈子里，这被视为理所当然，但确实有些人只会说德语，或者英语太差而尽量避免开口。

comments.author: aveao
comments.textZhHtml:
这不是真的。我现在住在慕尼黑和汉堡，你100%需要一定程度的德语来驾驭生活，很多人实际上没有会话水平的英语。我经常说，常见的路径往往很容易用英语导航（例如预订火车票、拨打电话线），但相邻的流程（例如为未完成的座位预订提出退款请求、取消电话合同）通常需要与用德语的人交谈。医生可能被标记为说英语，但这并不意味着他们的接待处说英语。

在德国柏林可能是一个不同的故事，然后还有整个例如。荷兰，但我知道法国的情况并不比这里好。

---

话虽如此，B1在德语中是一个困难的级别。能够用不完全正确的德语进行足够流利的交谈（这足以应付日常生活）并不一定足以通过测试，你确实需要记住数千个单词的语法性别，同时真正擅长以各种语法形式应用它们（例如，同一个单词的词尾变化根据其使用方式而有所不同）。

comments.author: stnikolauswagne
comments.textZhHtml:
有一种奇怪的现象，我无法用语言来形容，但其核心是：

虽然有很大一部分德国本土人口能说流利的英语，但有一小部分（通常）服务人员......却不会。过去几周，有多名家具搬运工来到我家，与他们沟通的唯一方式是蹩脚的德语，因为他们没有英语技能，而我的（克罗地亚语？塞尔维亚语？）技能为零。

有一次，我陷入了一生中最奇怪的境地，我将电话代表的德语指令翻译成英语，交给搬家公司的值班领导，这样他就可以用母语将它们交给搬运工。

comments.author: bulbar
comments.textZhHtml:
这就是为什么你应该学习你所居住国家的语言的原因。

在德国指责人们不会说英语是很奇怪的。学习德语怎么样？

comments.author: fransje26
comments.textZhHtml:
> 在德国责怪别人不说英语挺奇怪的。不如自己学学德语吧？

正如发帖人所写：

>> 有次我遇到了人生中最离谱的情况，我居然把电话客服的德语指示翻译成英语[..]

这说明他其实会说德语。不过搬家工人不会。

stories.title: John Deere owners will get the right to repair equipment under FTC settlement
stories.titleZh: 根据联邦贸易委员会和解协议，约翰迪尔所有者将获得维修设备的权利
stories.author: djoldman
summaryZh: It looks like John Deere owners can soon feel free to fix their own machines.The Federal Trade Commission and attorneys general from several states secured a right-to-repair settlement Wednesday with agriculture equipment giant Deere & Co. — commonly known as John Deere — that requires the company to let farmers and independent shops fix their own equipment.The Illinois-based manufacturer has faced complaints for years for withholding the software needed for repairs and forcing customers to use authorized dealers instead of independent ones. This marks the second right-to-repair settlement Deere has reached this year, following a separate $99 million class-action settlement with farmers in April. Though the class-action compensated consumers, the FTC’s settlement instead requires Deere to make its repair services available to equipment owners and independent shops.The FTC and attorneys general from Arizona, Illinois, Michigan, Minnesota and Wisconsin brought the antitrust lawsuit in January 2025, arguing that Deere had illegally restricted farmers and independent shops that might otherwise service them from repairing farm equipment such as tractors. Deere also makes engines and equipment for forestry, landscaping and construction. Under the order filed in Illinois, Deere will now be required to make diagnostic and repair tools available to equipment owners and independent repair shops, not only its own network of authorized dealers. It also prevents Deere dealers from retaliating against equipment owners or repair shops who choose to fix their own equipment instead of paying for Deere’s services. The order is headed to Judge Iain D. Johnston for his approval.

“For too long, Arizona farmers and independent mechanics have been at the mercy of Deere’s monopoly over repair tools, forced to wait — and pay — for authorized dealers just to fix broken tractors and other equipment,” Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes said in a statement Wednesday. Deere must pay $1 million collectively to the five states for antitrust enforcement costs and will be subject to strict compliance oversight for the next 10 years. In the complaint, the FTC argued that Deere provides a service software tool to authorized dealers but does not provide the full version to equipment owners or independent shops. Deere had said the lawsuit was baseless, denied that its distribution of service tools was anticompetitive and argued that it could not monopolize services since it does not directly provide them.

comments.author: Cider9986
comments.textZhHtml:

comments.author: srejk
comments.textZhHtml: 我欣赏他所代表的一切 - 他几乎在所有问题上都站在正确的一边。我只是希望他能制作出更加简洁有效的视频。

comments.author: darksim905
comments.textZhHtml: 您真的想要比他已经制作的视频更简洁的视频吗？尽管在当今人们的注意力集中时间有限的今天，这些视频可能看起来很长，但他的视频非常棒，而且切题。他确实忽略了围绕许多问题的更多复杂性和戏剧性。他有时会胡言乱语吗？也许吧，但有时谈话速度太快，以至于我自己的研究还没完成一半，视频就结束了。这是来自自从他在您自己的 VoIP 服务器上发布有关旋转的视频以及他还在纽约时就一直关注他的人。

comments.author: Zancarius
comments.textZhHtml:
“人类苦难的叙述”（假设我记得大概的标题）是他早期的视频之一，在我的脑海中挥之不去，我永远不会忘记。这是对硬件主板诊断显然被最终用户滥用这一事实的半开玩笑的认可，这是一个乏味且常常吃力不讨好的过程。

我从观看他的视频中学到了很多东西，而且我什至不进行主板维修！心理学、商业、人类状况……

comments.author: cgyvbunji
comments.textZhHtml: 我个人认为，如果他这样做，人们就不会注意到。我的经验是，当你向人们抱怨数字版权时，除非你向他们介绍所有的来龙去脉及其影响，否则他们只会呆呆地看着。

comments.author: forinti
comments.textZhHtml: 向公众通报情况是一场艰苦的战斗。大多数人只是不理解，也不愿意理解这些问题，直到问题真正发生在他们身上，例如购买京东的农民。

comments.author: wickedsight
comments.textZhHtml: 我是那些大多会忽略他的视频的人之一，因为它们的长度以及它们给我的“老人对事情愤怒咆哮”的氛围。

comments.author: FatherOfCurses
comments.textZhHtml: 每个人都应该像他一样愤怒，这样公司们就不会一直坑我们了。

comments.author: tsumnia

comments.author: sneakymichael

同感。我很认同他的理念，这些年也看过他的一些视频，但他那种冗长迂回的表达方式实在让我提不起劲。

不过，我还是非常高兴他能坚持做他所做的事，做他自己。

comments.textZhHtml:
我在这里分享他的视频，但似乎没有人看到它们和/或关心投票；我得到的只是蟋蟀。这让我很困惑。示例：

comments.textZhHtml: HN的受众更喜欢文字而不是视频；与其他网站相比，视频在这里很少能取得好成绩。

comments.author: spacedcowboy
comments.textZhHtml:
确实如此。我从来不看视频帖子。我可以在几秒钟内略读一个文字网站，看看是否值得仔细阅读。但视频做不到这一点，而且里面总是有广告，不管是不是作者自己加的。我讨厌广告。

而且这不仅仅是HN的问题，视频根本无法很好地替代一篇好文章。视频可以补充一篇好文章，但无法取代它。

comments.author: pchristensen
comments.textZhHtml:
如果你有YouTube Premium，你可以用他们的Gemini AI来总结视频。自从发现这个功能后，我“消费”的视频数量大幅增加，因为通过摘要我就能大致判断这个视频是否值得真正去看。

我在YouTube上花费的总时长减少了，但我互动的视频数量可能增加了2到3倍。就像在HN上略读一样！

stories.title: Chatto is now open source
stories.author: speckx
summaryZh: Hot damn. This is the big one. I’m happy to announce that Chatto, the group and team chat application that I’ve been working on for the past year or so, is now officially Open Source, and available for anyone to self-host. The fastest way to give it a try is through Homebrew: brew install chattocorp/tap/chatto chatto init chatto run See Chatto’s Getting Started Guide for details. Or stick around to hear more! Chat Just Got Real Chatto aims to be the group chat application that you actually enjoy using. You’re probably familiar with the one that rhymes with “knack”, or the one that rhymes with “beams”, or the one that rhymes with “this gourd”. Chatto is just like those. Except you’re going to love how compact and snappy it is. And that it’s Open Source. And you can just self-host it. For free, too! (A weird thing to write, but the OSS chat app space has become very weird in many ways!) This is what it looks like: If you want to see it in action, drop by the Chatto HQ Community! It’s designed to be extremely easy to self-host on your own infrastructure. In its most basic shape, you just run the executable, and that’s it. It even serves its own frontend! It’s very light on resources, and probably has the snappiest frontend that you’ve ever used in an app like this. It puts data protection and privacy first, with all personal and chat data fully encrypted at rest with per-user keys that get shredded when a user decides to delete their account. Each Chatto server powers a single community, with no federation of data between servers, nor any third-party tracking or analytics. If you want to hang out in multiple servers at once, the client will simply connect to all of them directly. If you want to host multiple communities, just spin up multiple Chatto processes. Easy! Chatto comes with full support for voice and video calls, with screen-sharing, built in. Calls are fully end-to-end encrypted and will scale to as many participants as your infrastructure can handle. And you can use it today, for free, by self-hosting it on your own server. Binaries are available for Linux (x86_64 and ARM64), macOS, and Windows; head over to the Chatto Self-Hosting Documentation site to get started. Chatto Cloud If you prefer someone else to take care of the hosting, I’m also happy to announce that Chatto Cloud will soon enter public beta. Chatto Cloud’s offering is very simple: it provides paid hosting for Chatto servers — and that’s it. No premium subscriptions, no ads, no icky bits. Just hosting. And it’s really good hosting! Chatto Cloud is launching with fully European and European-owned infrastructure, with more regions slated for launch in early 2027. Every Chatto server on Chatto Cloud benefits from automatic scaling, nightly backups of all data, and zero-downtime version upgrades. There’s no lock-in; servers hosted through Chatto Cloud are 100% compatible with self-hosted ones, and you can pack up your data and move into or out of Chatto Cloud at any time. If you want to get notified about the start of the beta, please see the end of this post for a low-volume newsletter you can subscribe to. What’s Next for Chatto Chatto is now at version 0.4. I consider it stable enough for production use, but there are a few important features still missing — head over to the Chatto Roadmap if you want an overview. The focus for Chatto 0.5 will be on additional safety features (content reporting and moderation) as well as polishing the client, particularly its multi-server functionality. I have some fun stuff planned for this that I can’t wait to put into people’s hands. I expect Chatto to hit 1.0.0 in about 6-12 months. Until then, there may still be breaking changes, even though I’ll be trying to keep them to a minimum. If you do decide to self-host, please be ready to update to new versions as they are released. Get in Touch It’s been an exciting journey so far and I’m looking forward to finding out what’s ahead. If you’re self-hosting Chatto, I’m super eager to hear from you about your experience — please don’t hesitate to head over to the Chatto HQ community and get in touch. Also please feel free to drop by and say hello if you’re interested in Chatto for your company, Open Source project, or similar. I’d love to learn more about your requirements, and help you get set up. Links Chatto HQ Community - we have a #self-hosting support channel! Chatto Self-Hosting Documentation GitHub Repository Chatto on Bluesky Newsletter If you want to be notified about new releases or the start of Chatto Cloud’s beta, you’re invited to subscribe to the Chatto announcements newsletter. It’s super low-volume (~1 email per month), and is only used for notifying you when exciting new stuff becomes available.

comments.author: wxw
comments.textZhHtml:
> 它的设计非常容易在您自己的基础设施上自行托管。

对此表示赞赏。根据文档：https://docs.chatto.run/，

> Chatto 以紧凑、独立的二进制形式提供

> 它使用 NATS，这是一个紧凑的消息代理，还附带内置流持久性引擎 [...] NATS 与 Chatto 一样易于配置，我们的大多数示例将向您展示如何配置。

> 您还可以配置外部与 S3 兼容的对象存储可供 Chatto 存储您的文件，我们强烈建议您这样做...

> 实际调用由 LiveKit (Apache-2.0) 提供支持，您需要将其与 Chatto 一起部署。与 NATS 一样，部署示例显示了所需的接线。

> ...

并感谢您在真正的指导下对其进行备份。伟大的项目。

comments.author: lofties
comments.textZhHtml: 哇，它正在使用 NATS！我十多年前广泛使用 NATS，很高兴听到它仍然存在。我们的基础设施在我们的机器群中出现了问题，但在某些小型机器上始终保持正常运行且没有任何抱怨的部分是 NATS。嗯，还有 Redis。没有任何抱怨。

comments.author: freakynit
comments.textZhHtml: 我们正在构建一些 B2B 的东西，预计具有足够好的规模......我们必须在 kafka、nats、redpanda 和rabbitMQ 之间进行选择......我们继续选择 NATS ....不知道为什么，但是，当我们浏览所有文档（包括设置和操作）时，NATS 感觉是正确的。

comments.author: bobkb
comments.textZhHtml:
我们也正处于同样的决策阶段，正在RabbitMQ、Kafka、NATS以及一些基于Redis构建的解决方案之间做选择。

NATS最终用起来效果好吗？

comments.author: freakynit
comments.textZhHtml: 太棒了。我们已经测试了每分钟500万条消息的规模，每条消息小于1KiB，这个规模还算可以。请注意，NATS 可以轻松处理100倍于此的规模。这只是我们在初期测试的数据。

comments.author: bobkb
comments.textZhHtml:
哇！

我有个愚蠢的问题——据我了解，NATS 作为“消息”管道/总线工作得非常好。有没有办法也能获得类似 Redis 的缓存功能呢？这有可能实现吗？

comments.author: freakynit
comments.textZhHtml:
JetStream（不是 NATS）内置了键值存储。相关帖子（旧帖，来自2023年）：https://github.com/nats-io/nats.go/discussions/1507

有人在2025年做了一些基准测试：

    nats bench kv put --size="128" --msgs 1000000 --storage file
    1m3s → Pub stats: 15,656 msgs/sec ~ 1.91 MB/sec
    
    nats bench kv get --size="128" --msgs 1000000
    59s → Sub stats: 16,720 msgs/sec ~ 2.04 MB/sec
不过我个人还没用过这个。

comments.author: moeffju
comments.textZhHtml: 受Chatto启发，我也用NATS（+Go）技术栈构建了一些原型，体验简直太棒了。感觉这项技术被严重低估了 :D

comments.author: hendrikmans

NATS及其内置的Jetstream流持久化引擎简直是奇迹。我太喜欢它们了。而且如果你的应用本身是用Go编写的，你完全可以直接嵌入它们，以应对简单的部署场景（就像Chatto那样。）

comments.textZhHtml: 我用livekit跑了类似的东西，全都部署在Hetzner上。对于一群人同时使用来说，成本低得令人难以置信。

comments.author: millsau
comments.textZhHtml: 看起来是一个很棒的项目，希望让 AI 机器人能够相互交谈

comments.author: pjg
comments.textZhHtml:
遗留应用程序继续存在（尽管不再有用）的原因之一是第三方搭配。

大多数公司不愿意从 Slack 切换的原因是因为他们有外部第三方，即他们的合作伙伴与 slack 内的外部渠道连接。更改内部 DM/IM 系统将消除他们与同一系统中的合作伙伴进行通信的能力。

也许有一个插件，可以将 slack 消息转发到 Chatto，并最终实现双向，即 Chatto 消息可以转发到 Slack。

comments.author: mertbio

stories.title: GPT‑Live
stories.author: logickkk1
summaryZh: OpenAI 推出 GPT-Live，可能为实时交互型 AI 模型或服务。
该发布在 Hacker News 上引发高度关注，得分 730，评论数达 493。
highlightsZh: OpenAI 新产品 GPT-Live 正式发布。
Hacker News 社区高度关注，互动热烈。
可能涉及实时 AI 推理或流式交互能力。
体现 OpenAI 在 AI 产品化方面的持续布局。

comments.author: simonw
comments.textZhHtml:
我有几周的预览权限。非常好。我在遛狗时进行了一次持续整整一个小时的对话，对我的一个项目进行了一些很好的头脑风暴。

最好的功能是它可以在后台将问题委托给 GPT-5.5，因此你不再局限于落后前沿几年的语音模型。

不过，我确实报告了一个有趣的错误：当我还在说话时，它打断了我，并嘲笑我的（不是真正打算的）笑话！值得庆幸的是，他们似乎已经限制了这种行为，感觉有点粗鲁和居高临下。

comments.author: 100ms
comments.textZhHtml: 是否响应个性设置？我非常不想要假人工智能女友，但我确实从语音模式中获得了很多价值。期待尝试这个，但希望它不是一个令人毛骨悚然的过度混乱（像芝麻）。预计他们会继续加倍投入假人工智能女朋友的方法，因为我想要的东西可能不会在任何地方提高参与度

comments.author: Someone1234
comments.textZhHtml:
我在他们之前的尝试中也发现了这一点。

我已经将我的聊天个性设置精简到没有任何绒毛。我希望语音更类似于《星际迷航》中的计算机，而不是像你所说的人工智能朋友，但之前它被调整得过于风度翩翩/像朋友一样。

comments.author: 100ms
comments.textZhHtml: 《星际迷航》计算机语音模型是我还没有遇到过的东西，我反复看了:)这不是关于特定的声音，而是他们成功地在声音中完美地捕捉到了“我是一个实用工具”。我们的现代朋友不想被视为实用工具，而是希望建立自己的信任和代理权，这对我来说是一个巨大的问题。

comments.author: 100ms
comments.textZhHtml:
我想尝试使用dots.tts ( https://huggingface.co/spaces/rednote-hilab/dots.tts ) 进行语音克隆，结果相当不错，但可能不够快，无法在准实时基础上使用：

输入剪辑：https://vocaroo.com/19QtEPtwTjOS

提示文字：该复制器提供 14 种番茄汤。搭配米饭、搭配蔬菜、波莲风格、搭配面食指定热食或冷食。

输出：https://vocaroo.com/1f3XuQQoSzwB

comments.author: jldugger
comments.textZhHtml:
我认为这里的要求不是听起来像 Majel Barrett，而是要保持输出极其简洁和不引人注目。

有一些研究表明，新手喜欢 LLM 输出很长，但专家讨厌它。举个例子，我的任务是使用一些代理 PM 工具来编写规范，它不断生成这些大页长的输出，并带有“HBR 语音”段落长项目符号的粗体摘要。即：

> 适当大小的硬性，并观察一个开放式边缘。大规模认可 DRI 的简化：放弃每个警报的运行手册要求（为高优先级集保留 1-2 个仅用于诊断的运行手册），并在现有的每周事件 + 每月运营审查上实现持久性 - 无需新的治理。单一范围蔓延风险是覆盖链（间隙由缺席定义）；将其与实际的、已经错过的面向客户的中断所证明的差距联系起来，而不是主动寻找差距。消除所有权差距（例如 foo-bar，没有明确的所有者）是有限的范围内工作。

每次迭代都有数十个这样的工作。我无法想象尝试通过语音来处理这个问题，我会在第二句话后走神。

comments.author: ijidak
comments.textZhHtml:
当语音模型开始变得杂乱无章时，我想到了 C-3PO。当欧文叔叔让 3PO 闭嘴时，3PO 说：“闭嘴，先生。”

甚至在某些场景中 Data 也做了类似的事情。

现在体验起来很有趣。

comments.author: elzbardico
comments.textZhHtml: 哈哈哈。HBR语音。谢了兄弟，你形容得太到位了。我终于知道这叫什么了。

comments.author: bityard
comments.textZhHtml:
我只想说，哇。这是一个相当令人难以置信的结果。我不知道现在可以用如此短的音频片段克隆声音。

从 8 岁起，我就一直梦想着与 Enterprise-D 计算机交谈。中年危机的我仍然有这个梦想，但连接到家庭助理，所以它实际上也可以做有用的事情。几个月前，我四处寻找 Majel 声音的“干净”样本作为计算机，但运气不佳。尽管有三部电视剧和几部电影，但几乎所有这些都有一定程度的背景噪音、嘟嘟声、杂音或曲速核心嗡嗡声。 （就像这本书一样。）可能有现代方法可以在不影响她声音的情况下清理这些内容，但我还没有深入研究。

有一些由 Majel Barrett 叙述的有声读物，但显然她作为计算机的角色是正确的配音，所以这些书不会是很好的源材料。

还有一些带有一些样本的游戏/CD-ROM（全百科），但他们没有费心为那个 lofi 进行后处理。 Enterprise-D 电脑感觉。事后可能可以相当忠实地复制，但我对音频后期处理只知道一点点。

据她的儿子（Rod Roddenberry）说，Majel 坐在工作室里录制音频样本，专门为了有一天为未来的《星际迷航》故事克隆她的声音。然而，这些尚未公开发布。 （可能永远不会，但我可以梦想，不是吗？）

编辑：我玩了你的样本，生成输出音频所需的时间非常残酷。对于交互式使用来说太慢了。不过，这也许是 HF 托管应用程序的限制。

comments.author: 100ms
comments.textZhHtml: 如果你看了他们的GitHub自述文件和代码，就会发现可以把语音克隆步骤分离出来并加以缓存，而且他们还有一种流式变体，首包延迟很低。我只在Hugging Face上体验过。它对某些罕见口音的效果出奇的好，而且对较长的输入片段响应也很出色。

comments.author: pseudosavant
comments.textZhHtml: 对于如此少量的输入来说，这是相当令人难以置信的。让我想亲自查看dots.tts！

comments.author: andai
comments.textZhHtml: 我只是告诉他们像贾维斯一样说话。我尝试了“冷酷逻辑”提示，但它把他们变成了一群混蛋。

comments.author: Melonai
comments.textZhHtml:
我也注意到了这一点，我猜训练数据中的内容表明，当某些文本自我识别为“冷酷、强硬的逻辑”时，它还会呈现出刻薄的语气。此外，即使给出的输入或多或少有效，它也会使响应更有可能出现不一致。不久前，我测试了用“硬逻辑”提示向模型提供一些想法，然后获取其自己的输出，恢复对话并再次给出自己的陈述，使其与刚刚自己做出的陈述不一致。这主要只是幽默，并没有说明太多，毕竟这两种陈述可能在某种程度上都是错误的，但陈述本身更多的是一种没有完全正确答案的观点，显示出这种不同意的倾向。

我想当我们告诉模型使用“冷逻辑”时，他们不会纯粹从定义上解释这一点，更重要的是这个陈述通常意味着什么，这通常是两个人之间的分歧，一个人试图利用所谓的逻辑来反对和诽谤对方，通常实际上是这样争论是出于情感，而不是他们声称使用的逻辑。这种情况的发生可能足以使模型响应呈现出平均的色调。这就是我关于为什么这种情况至少会发生的理论。

我明确提示我的小型本地模型要引导这种能量。当我刚刚问这周是否会下雨时，这是让他们直接吐出来而不是大喊大叫有关温度和湿度适当的活动的少数方法之一。

stories.title: Chat Control 1.0 and 2.0 Explained
stories.author: gasull
summaryZh: The temporary, voluntary scanning regime — adopted in 2021, rejected by Parliament in March 2026, expired in April 2026, and reinstated on 9 July 2026 through an unprecedented revival. Jul 14, 2021 Temporary derogation adopted Regulation (EU) 2021/1232 creates a temporary exception to the ePrivacy Directive, giving providers a legal basis to voluntarily scan private messages for child sexual abuse material. Originally set to expire 3 August 2024. Apr 29, 2024 First extension With the permanent regulation (Chat Control 2.0) nowhere near agreement, the derogation is extended until 3 April 2026. Dec 18, 2025 Commission proposes second extension The Commission proposes extending the derogation by another two years, to April 2028. Mar 2, 2026 LIBE committee rejects the extension In a surprise vote, the Parliament's civil liberties committee rejects the draft extension by 38 votes to 28. Mar 11, 2026 Parliament adopts a protective position The plenary votes 458–103 for a compromise: extend to 2027, but only with targeted and proportionate detection of known content, no end-to-end encrypted communications, and limiting scanning to suspected users or groups identified by the competent judicial authority. Mid-Mar 2026 Trilogue on the extension collapses The Council rejects Parliament's conditions and shows no flexibility in negotiations; talks on the extension break down. Mar 26, 2026 Parliament rejects the extension outright 311 MEPs vote against extending the derogation (228 in favour, 92 abstentions). The critical Amendment 34, rejecting automated assessment of unknown photos and texts, passes by a single vote (307–306). Apr 4, 2026 Chat Control 1.0 expires The legal ground for voluntary, indiscriminate scanning ends. Google, Meta, Microsoft, and Snap state they will continue scanning private messages regardless. Jun 26, 2026 Council moves to resurrect the expired law EU ambassadors agree to push a temporary revival — unprecedented, as Parliament's rejection was considered final. Because an expired regulation cannot be extended, the Council proposes a formally new law with identical content via an expedited procedure. Jul 2, 2026 Council adopts its position The Council adopts its position on the "new" regulation via written procedure. Jul 7, 2026 Urgency procedure approved Parliament voted 331–303 (11 abstentions) to fast-track the expired derogation, skipping the responsible Committee. A binding vote follows on Thursday, 9 July, where an absolute majority of 361 MEPs is needed to stop it. Jul 9, 2026 Parliament greenlights Chat Control 1.0 Chat Control 1.0 passed despite a majority of voting MEPs opposing it (314:276, 17 abstentions): the votes to reject fell short of the 361-vote absolute majority required, ushering in suspicionless mass scanning of private communications until 2028. A separate amendment to restrict scanning to suspects identified by the judiciary won 322 votes but also missed the absolute majority, while an exemption for end-to-end encrypted services passed. Regrettably, President Roberta Metsola's brazen and undemocratic actions jeopardise the legitimacy of the EU (source).

The permanent CSA Regulation — proposed in 2022, deadlocked for years, and still unagreed after five rounds of trilogue negotiations. Encryption remains the red line. May 11, 2022 Commission proposes the CSA Regulation Home Affairs Commissioner Ylva Johansson unveils a proposal for a permanent regulation making detection and reporting of child sexual abuse material a legal requirement for platforms — including a requirement to bypass end-to-end encryption. Nov 2023 Parliament adopts a protective mandate No scanning of end-to-end encrypted services, detection limited to visual material, judicial warrants targeted at specific suspects, and no mandatory age verification. Oct 2025 Germany breaks the Council deadlock After years of Council deadlock, Germany announces it will vote against mandatory suspicionless scanning. The Danish presidency drops detection orders and shifts to risk assessment and mitigation obligations for providers, while proposing to make the voluntary suspicionless scanning (interim regulation) permanent. Nov 26, 2025 Council endorses its position The Council adopts the softened Danish compromise, opening trilogue negotiations. Critics note the text still allows “voluntary” suspicionless detection and imposes broad risk-mitigation duties, including mandatory age verification, that could reshape private messaging in practice. Dec 2025 – May 2026 Four trilogue rounds Negotiations between Parliament, Council, and Commission take place on 9 December 2025, 26 February, 16 April, and 11 May 2026 — without agreement on the core issues. Jun 10, 2026 Council's own lawyers raise the alarm The Council Legal Service states that the "voluntary" scanning proposal still constitutes generalised scanning of communications — incompatible with Article 7 of the EU Charter absent reasonable suspicion and prior judicial authorisation. Jun 29, 2026 "Final" trilogue fails The fifth trilogue, billed as the last with adoption targeted for July, produces no deal. Negotiators cannot agree on making suspicionless scanning permanent, as requested by Council. Progress is reported on excluding mandatory age verification, but agreement is postponed and talks continue under the incoming Irish presidency.

stories.title: GLM 5.2 and the coming AI margin collapse
stories.titleZh: GLM 5.2 和即将到来的人工智能利润率崩溃
stories.author: martinald
summaryZh: This is a two part series focusing on what I believe is perhaps the least understood upcoming shift in AI economics. If you've enjoyed this and want to be notified about the second post, please feel free to sign up for my newsletter. The real DeepSeek moment is upon us What feels like decades ago, markets recoiled at DeepSeek's R1 model. The theory being that given the underlying V3 model reportedly cost under $6m to train, the market therefore thought the huge investment in capex for model training was over, and thus the stock price of Nvidia et al collapsed overnight. Of course, this was a hugely poor read of where the costs actually lie in AI. Training - while no doubt capex intensive - is a fixed, up-front cost. You spend hundreds of millions to train a model, then you are "done".[1] Inference, on the other hand, scales with your demand. It has genuine marginal costs. I've written about this at length over the past year or so. Again, the mainstream understanding of this - that the API costs the providers charge are their real costs is mistaken. Indeed, when Anthropic/OpenAI charge $25/MTok for inference, my napkin maths suggests that this is probably something like 90% gross margin on the cost of compute vs the rack rate. It may be a bit higher, or a bit lower (OpenAI's leaked financials suggest a ~60% gross margin on revenue, but this no doubt includes a lot of other costs like support, payment processing and other services they offer), but the whole business model of frontier AI labs is in short to spend a large amount of money on salaries on compute to train a model, then amortise that cost over a lot of very profitable inference. If you can amortise that cost over enough inference you turn from profitable on a COGS basis to... actually profitable.

I have been playing around with GLM5.2 from Z.ai for the last couple of weeks. I believe GLM5.2 is the first model that reaches the "bar" of a genuine open weights competitor to Opus and GPT (at the time of writing, the latest version of GPT was 5.5 - future models no doubt will exceed this). It's genuinely very good and hard for me to tell the difference between Opus - my daily driver and it. I've found that it is slow because of the amount of thinking it tends to do. For non interactive agentic tasks (like reviewing PRs in the background) which aren't time critical this is a non issue, but for interactive use it is definitely a tad too slow to keep my attention. This also somewhat reduces the cost effectiveness of it (more thinking means more tokens, which increases costs). It also doesn't have vision support. It's funny how quickly I've gone from basically never wanting to use vision (because it was so inaccurate, I'd often pause sessions when I caught it using vision), to using it all the time - since Opus 4.7 introduced far higher resolution vision capabilities. It's genuinely frustrating it not being able to read image-based PDFs, screenshots and design files. I'm sure they have a more multimodal model in the works, but this is a significant weakness against the frontier labs. Secondly, and something I really didn't expect to be a blocker, is the lack of/poor web search capabilities. It turns out that nearly every agentic session does a lot of web searching for looking up items. Z.ai provides a replacement MCP for web search, but it's pretty awful and slow. Fireworks doesn't provide any, though they gave me a very vague answer saying they are always looking to improve products. I would take that as no plans personally, but let's see. I've managed to somewhat work around this by telling the agent to use a CLI based web search like ddgr, but this is a real weakness right now. I am very bullish on the potential of 3rd party web search APIs. This is actually a huge gap in what open weights model providers can offer, and it turns out great web search capabilities are essential for many agentic tasks. Regardless, this no doubt will be solved with time - there are many people building web search indexes and it just requires the right partnerships and plumbing in place. Drop in replacement Where it gets really scary for the frontier labs is how easy it is to migrate to open weights models. Both Z.ai and Fireworks offer both an OpenAI compatible and Anthropic compatible endpoint. This makes it absolutely trivial to use with Claude Code and Codex. You just set the base URL to point to your inference provider, give it the API key and tell it to use GLM5.2. Given Anthropic recently announced (then backtracked) on charging API rates for claude -p non interactive agentic use, you will find for many/most of those use cases you can just drop in GLM instead. And for interactive use, apart from the lack of vision and slow(er) speed[2], it was genuinely almost impossible for me to realise I wasn't using Opus in Claude Code. This is not Microsoft or Salesforce like lock in, where you need to spend years planning a migration. The switching costs are incredibly low, and I would argue that are actually far less than trying to keep up on all the policy and term changes that the frontier lab models tend to scramble around with. It's possible that Claude Code will make it harder to use 3rd party providers, but there are many good open source options (like Codex itself and OpenCode, amongst dozens). One concern I do hear from enterprise is data privacy and security. There is no doubt that using Z.ai's official API and subscription is almost certainly a non-starter, with their terms being at best weak and the deep connection to Mainland China. But of course, with open weights being open there are many other providers in the market, many with proper contractual provisions. And, if that isn't enough, you can of course host in on premises yourself, which actually opens up even more sensitive data - that couldn't be sent to any third party - to Opus-quality agentic workflows. Cost savings The going rate for GLM5.2 seems to be around the $4.40/MTok mark. This is less than 20% of the retail price of Opus and ~15% the cost of GPT5.5. Now, given it does use more tokens for a given task, this isn't a totally apples to apples comparison. But I'd be very surprised if it wasn't more than 50% cheaper for nearly all workflows, for a very similar level of quality. In terms of subscriptions, Z.ai offers a "coding plan" subscription which mirrors the plans you'd see from Anthropic and OpenAI, but with a higher claimed usage limit. I expect for most professional use the very lax terms around training and data retention will make this a difficult sell, but if the frontier labs were to try and increase pricing substantially I can see it being a credible option for those that are budget-conscious. I expect these costs for GLM5.2 to come down significantly over the coming months as well, as more optimisation is done to the serving stack(s). Wafer wrote an interesting write up of their efforts to run it on AMD hardware. They suggest that it is 2.75x cheaper per token to run inference on AMD vs Nvidia Blackwell. Part two is where this gets interesting - what a collapse in inference margins actually does to the industry, and who is likely to win and lose. I'd keep Bezos's famous "your margin is my opportunity" line in mind. If you'd like me to drop it in your inbox the moment it's out, sign up to the newsletter - or grab the RSS feed if that's more your thing. Disclosure - Fireworks kindly gave me some free credit to experiment with GLM to help write this article.

comments.author: fny
comments.textZhHtml:
我不相信原始成本很重要：

1.自云出现以来，计算成本大幅下降，但超大规模企业仍然拥有丰厚的利润。

2.存在许多开源办公套件，但没有一个可以与无处不在的 gsuite 或 Office 竞争。 GitHub、Slack 都是类似的例子。

3.尽管免费替代品早已存在，但 Windows 和 macOS 都在家庭桌面领域占据主导地位。

4.许多以前的开源基础设施组件（如 Redis 和 Elastic Search）都有 Apache 的同类组件，但它们仍然拥有健康的利润。

我理解利润崩溃的论点，但我没有看到任何历史类似物。企业似乎愿意为服务保证、集成以及可以起诉的人支付高额费用。

没有人会因为再次购买 IBM 而被解雇。

comments.author: nostrademons
comments.textZhHtml:
在试图回忆历史类似事件时，存在着巨大的幸存者偏差，因为在每一次利润锐减和竞争使该行业成为商品业务的情况下，大的专有名称都不再属于我们。 不过，这里是一些示例的选择：

1.内存芯片利润在 80 年代大幅下滑，以至于英特尔完全退出了内存芯片业务。 当时，他们更多地被认为是一家内存芯片公司，而不是一家微处理器公司。

2。面对更便宜的 IBM PC 克隆产品和 MS Windows 软件的爆炸式增长，高端工作站的利润大幅下降。 这直接导致了SGI、Sun、Symbolics、Lucid、LMI等的消亡。

3。 HP-UX、IRIX、AIX 和 SCO Unix 等专有 UNIX 变体基本上已经完全消失，取而代之的是 Windows 和 MacOS 等成本较低的专有操作系统，或者 Linux 和 BSD 的开源后代。

4。许多商业数据库供应商，如 Oracle、dBase、Sybase、FoxPro 和 Microsoft (SQL Server and Access) 发现自己面临来自 PostGres、MySQL 和 SQLite 的利润压力。 Oracle 幸存下来要归功于其庞大的安装基础和法律部门，而 Microsoft 幸存下来是因为他们可以从其操作系统和 Office 垄断中进行交叉补贴，但 dBase、Sybase 和 FoxPro 已不再属于我们。

comments.author: cb321
comments.textZhHtml: 这是一个很好的列表。 虽然我确信可以添加许多家公司，但我发帖只是为了包括 DEC https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_Equipment_Corporation，其 `vt50/vt100/vtXX` 想法可能会在软件中永久存在于我们身边，除非像 Arcan (https://arcan-fe.com/) 这样的东西能够成功。

comments.author: corford
comments.textZhHtml:
这是政治。

美国在 20 世纪 80 年代和 1990 年代初存在企业问题，当时国际竞争激烈。

随着 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1986_U.S.%E2%80%93Japan_Semico... 和 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plaza_Accord。

自由贸易协定中的反规避条款等进一步加速了这种趋势（参见 Cory Doctrow 最近强调的这一点：https://pluralistic.net/2026/01/01/39c3/），然后在全球金融危机后的 ZIRP/宽松货币时代火上浇油，最终以刺激措施的火箭筒而达到顶峰后新冠疫情爆发。

我最好的猜测是，我们现在将见证约 20 年的缓慢放松。你已经可以在以下方面看到这种迹象：世界其他地区/新兴市场股票的表现优于标准普尔指数，国债收益率与其他“避险”主权债券（例如瑞士债券）不同，金价上涨，欧洲开始认真对待德拉吉报告的调查结果，欧洲国防支出增加，中国开始在最近的伊朗/美国冲突中表现得像“房间里的成年人”等等。重新主张在过去约 30 年里为了主要美国的利益而自愿淡化的主权。

comments.author: nostrademons
comments.textZhHtml:
在此处评论的其他地方，其他人评论道“个人可能[转向新模型]，但不是组织。”

这很说明问题。 组织被迫更新技术、转向更有竞争力的供应商并削减成本的机制是衰退。 其一，每家不这样做的企业都会破产，剩下的是有效采用技术的效率更高的企业。

自 2009 年以来，我们还没有经历过真正的衰退。（2020 年是一个奇怪的例子，因为它是它实际上是由政府法令引发的，因此它实际上杀死了一些高效但不幸的部门，同时没有采取任何措施来清理大公司的死木）。 下一个可能会很疯狂，因为经济充满了狗屎工作、狗屎公司和狗屎产品。

comments.author: Dumblydorr

不，由一种导致 1000 万人死亡的新型病原体引起。如果没有政府采取行动，情况会更糟。

comments.author: rdbl27

瑞典的案例彻底证伪了“如果没有政府干预，情况会糟糕得多”这一理论。

瑞典没有采取任何政府行动。完全没有。其死亡率与那些采取大规模干预措施的邻国相比，既没有更好，也没有更糟。

因此，无论如何，政府干预都是无关紧要的。

stories.title: Rewriting Bun in Rust
stories.author: afturner
summaryZh: Disclosure: Bun was acquired by Anthropic in December 2025. I and others on the Bun team work at Anthropic. I used a pre-release version of Claude Fable 5 for much of the Rust rewrite.Bun started as a line-for-line port of esbuild's JavaScript & TypeScript transpiler from Go to Zig. I wrote my first line of Zig on April 16, 2021. I bet on Zig after seeing the single-page Zig Language Reference on Hacker News and getting really excited about the low-level control and care for performance.From the start, Bun's scope was massive:JavaScript, TypeScript, and CSS transpiler, minifier, and bundlernpm-compatible package managerJest-like test runnerNode.js & TypeScript-compatible module resolutionHTTP/1.1 & WebSocket clientNode.js API implementations like fs, net, tls, and dozens of other modulesThe initial version of Bun was written by me in 1 year, in a cramped Oakland apartment, pre-LLM, in Zig. The default outcome for ambitiously-scoped projects like Bun is joining the graveyard of dead side projects on a GitHub profile page. Zig made Bun possible. I would never have been able to build this much in 1 year if it wasn't for Zig.Nowadays, Bun's CLI gets over 22 million monthly downloads. Popular tools like Claude Code and OpenCode bet on Bun as their runtime. Vercel, Railway, DigitalOcean and more have 1st-party support for Bun.Bun's scope has also been a challenge for stability. Here's a small sample of bugs we fixed in Bun v1.3.14:heap-use-after-free crash in node:zlib when calling .reset() on a zlib, Brotli, or Zstd stream while an async .write() is still in progress on the threadpooluse-after-free crash in node:zlib when an onerror callback issued a re-entrant write() followed by close() on native handlesuse-after-free crashes in node:http2 when re-entrant JS callbacks (e.g. session.request() inside a timeout listener, an options getter, or a write callback) triggered a hashmap rehash, invalidating internal stream pointersuse-after-free in UDPSocket.send() and sendMany() where user code in valueOf() or toString() callbacks could detach an ArrayBuffer between payload capture and the actual sendcrash and out-of-bounds read in Buffer#copy and Buffer#fill when a valueOf callback detaches or resizes the underlying ArrayBuffer during argument coercionheap out-of-bounds write in UDPSocket.sendMany() when the socket's connection state changed mid-iteration via user JS callbacksmemory leak in crypto.scrypt where the callback and protected password/salt buffers were never released when the output buffer allocation failedSSLWrapper.init leaked the strdup'd passphrase on error pathsmemory leak in tlsSocket.setSession() where each call leaked one SSL_SESSION (~6.5 KB per call) due to a missing SSL_SESSION_free after d2i_SSL_SESSIONmemory leak where fs.watch() watchers were never garbage collected after .close(), caused by a reference count underflow that permanently pinned each watcher as a GC rootdouble-free crash in the CSS parser when background-clip had vendor prefixes and multi-layer backgroundsDuplexUpgradeContext was never freed — a full leak per tls.connect({ socket: duplex })race condition crash in MessageEvent where the GC marker thread could observe a torn variant in m_data during concurrent access from a BroadcastChannel or MessagePortWe could have kept fixing these kinds of bugs one-off in perpetuity, but we owe it to our users counting on us to do better than that, and systematically prevent these kinds of bugs from recurring.What we were already doingWe patched the Zig compiler to add Address Sanitizer support. We run our test suite with ASAN on every commit.We ship Zig safety-checked ReleaseSafe builds on WindowsWe fuzz Bun's runtime APIs 24/7 using Fuzzilli, the JavaScript engine fuzzer used by V8 & JavaScriptCoreWe have a whole lot of end-to-end memory leak testsThis is more than many projects do.Just be really smart and don't make mistakes?Our bugfix list felt bad and I was tired of going to sleep worrying about crashes in Bun. I don't blame Zig for that - other users of Zig don't have the bugs we had, and mixing GC with manually-managed memory is an uncommon enough thing for software to need that no language really designs for it. We wouldn't have gotten this far if not for Zig, and I'll always be grateful. Until very recently, programming language choice was a one-way decision for a project like Bun.JavaScript is a garbage-collected language and modern JavaScript engines like JavaScriptCore (and V8) have strict rules around exception handling and the garbage collector. Zig, like C, doesn't manage memory for you and this is a tradeoff that for many projects is a great reason to use Zig. Zig does not have constructors/destructors, and most cleanup is expected to be written out explicitly at each call site with defer.For Bun, correctly handling the lifetimes of garbage-collected values and manually-managed values has been a major source of stability issues - most often small memory leaks and occasionally, crashes. Every memory allocation has to be meticulously reviewed. Where do these bytes get freed? How do we ensure it only gets freed once? Did we check for JavaScript exceptions properly? Is this garbage-collected pointer visible to the conservative stack scanner? Is this garbage collected memory or manually managed memory?For stability issues, knowing as early as possible is best. Fuzzing happens after code is merged. CI happens when code is pushed. Runtime safety checks & address sanitizer happens when code is run (hopefully in development, before CI).One common way to reduce this class of issue is to ensure cleanup code is always run exactly once for code that needs it. Zig is designed to be a simple language with no hidden control flow, and so it prefers the explicit defer keyword to run code at the end of a scope over C++'s implicit ~Destructor or Rust's implicit Drop.LanguageCleanupZigdefer, errdeferC++~Destructor, &&MoveRustDropFor Zig code, when exactly should we be running the cleanup code? If we're passing the same *T to many different functions, how do we know when it's no longer accessible and can be cleaned up? How does it work when some functions need to continue to reference the memory after the function is called? Our current approach is a mix of:arena lifetimes, where the scope of when it's accessible is clear (parser state doesn't escape the calling function and so AST nodes are a good choice there)reference-countingpay really close attentionMany projects opt to answer these kinds of questions through a style guide. TigerBeetle's TigerStyle is an example in Zig and Google's 31,000 word C++ style guide is another. The challenge with style guides is enforcement. How do you make sure the style guide is followed? Historically, code review was the answer with best-effort enforcement via linters & static analyzers.Having a rigid style guide with clear ownership expectations explicitly spelled out in the type system was a real option for Bun. Since Zig has no operator overloading, we would likely end up with a lot of code looking something like this:fn foo(a_ptr: SharedPtr(TCPSocket)) !void { const a: *TCPSocket = a_ptr.get(); defer a_ptr.deref();
const b = try do_something_with_a(a); defer b.deref();

comments.author: pier25
comments.textZhHtml:
我个人并不关心他们使用 AI 将 Bun 重写为 Rust。即使 1.4 不够好，随着时间的推移，它可能会变得更好。

让我回到 Node 的是看到过渡的处理是多么业余。

- 在 CVE 等方面，Zig 版本没有 LTS 支持。

- Zig 版本中废弃的博客文章中提到的 3MB 内存泄漏等巨大错误，基本上迫使人们使用 Rust 版本来修复生产中的应用程序。

- 与 Bun 的零参与社会对如此重大的决定。有一天，“别再闹了，我只是在玩这个”，几天后“yolo 合并到了 main”。

Jarred 基本上一直在运作，就好像他是一个孤独的黑客，致力于他的个人项目。

comments.author: egorfine
comments.textZhHtml:
> 与 Bun 社区的零参与

是的。这种转变的人性方面非常糟糕。 Bun 背后的人刚刚证明了他对社区的重视。

但我相信他会围绕这次重写建立另一个社区。毕竟，有很多人为“Rust 重写”欢呼。

直到新的最喜欢的语言出现。

comments.author: alexjurkiewicz
comments.textZhHtml: 付费客户可获得 LTS。是否有付费客户要求 Zig 分支机构 LTS？或者您是否期望开源维护者无特殊原因地做免费工作？

comments.author: pier25
comments.textZhHtml:
Java、Node 和 .NET 都有 LTS 版本，所有这些版本都可以免费使用。

> 或者您是否期望开源维护者无特殊原因地做免费工作？

免费工作？上次我听说 Anthropic 收购了 Bun。

comments.author: zamadatix
comments.textZhHtml:
假设他们仍然对外部采用率感兴趣，那么进入 Bun 的 LTS 版本可能是个好主意。他们在不到 3 年前发布了 1.0，并且收购花了大约 6 个月的时间来解决。考虑 LTS 版本将使许多发展较慢的地方能够担心陷入速度之中。

我不知道尝试在 Zig 版本停止的地方制作 LTS 版本是否有意义，特别是如果他们知道他们正在发布不同的解决方案和代码库来解决大部分安全和内存错误。让这个问题再稳定几个月，如果确实有需求的话，我们打算在年底之前发布它的 LTS。拥有 Java、Node 和 .NET 等 LTS 版本的所有好处，无需跳转到代码库的预移植版本，而代码库从一开始就没有被定位为 LTS 的良好版本。

如果 LTS 版本已经在计划中，那么最终的基于 Zig 的 LTS 将非常有意义。

comments.author: graemep
comments.textZhHtml:
> 假设他们仍然关心外部采用率。

如果不是的话，那将是一个放弃它的绝佳理由。

comments.author: raincole
comments.textZhHtml: 那么不仅仅是免费工作，而是您希望 Anthropic 付费来维护 Zig 版本...？

comments.author: woodruffw
comments.textZhHtml:
我不明白维护Bun的LTS版本怎么符合Anthropic的利益。他们似乎不太可能使用它，而且这会耗费他们的资金（以人力的形式）。

据我了解，Java和.NET提供公开的LTS版本，是因为使用这些LTS版本的大公司为他们提供了资金支持，而且Oracle和微软本质上是把LTS支持作为一种产品来销售的。我不知道Node为什么这么做，也许情况类似。但Bun的处境似乎与这三者都不同。

comments.author: Tadpole9181
comments.textZhHtml: 你是以Anthropic成员的身份在要求提供Zig的LTS版本吗？

comments.author: iknowstuff
comments.textZhHtml: 1.4 与 1.3 相比没有重大变化，那么为什么会有 LTS 以及对留在 1.3 的人们的任何保证呢？据我所知，所有已知的回归都已像任何其他版本一样得到修复

comments.author: nicce
comments.textZhHtml:
> - 对于有关 CVE 等的 Zig 版本没有 LTS 支持。

每个版本都会有大量的 CVE，并且需要付出很大的努力。例如。博客中存在内存问题的示例。最好认为 Zig 版本不存在安全性。使用风险自担。

>贾里德基本上一直在运作，就好像他是一个孤独的黑客，致力于他的个人项目。

但是，他们有权这样做。这是预料之中的，特别是如果公司拥有它的话。

comments.author: srdjanr
comments.textZhHtml: 他当然有权利这样做，没有人质疑这一点。用户和社区也有权利投诉、停止使用和支持Bun，因为他们不喜欢他的行为

comments.author: atonse
comments.textZhHtml:
如果存在任何类型的兼容性被破坏的情况，LTS 会更相关。他们仍然没有发布 1.4，尽管从各个指标来看，它似乎都运行得非常好，有大量的人使用 Claude 代码，一个月内没有出现任何回归。 在我看来，没有任何迹象表明他们在这里粗心。

事实上，他在每次代码更改、每一行上都有两个敌对的审阅者克劳德实例。我不知道有哪个人类团队会对每一行进行两次独立审查，除了编写航天飞机软件的人之外。

他们还修复了内存泄漏。它用什么语言编写有什么关系？归根结底，人们用它来运行打字稿代码等。

有多少 Bun 用户关心它是用 Zig 编写的？我当然不知道。我已经使用bun有两年了，我想我查过zig一次。它只是不相关。

它变得更稳定了吗？是的。更苗条？是的。性能更强？是的。有任何证据表明它的安全性较低吗？不。该代码已经发布两个月了。到现在为止，所有反对者都已经找到了确凿的证据。他们没有。您还需要多少证据来证明这是一次巨大的成功？

这是我们的新现实。代理商非常优秀，这样的项目是有可能实现的。这太令人兴奋了。

comments.author: pier25
comments.textZhHtml:
> 如果存在任何类型的兼容性被破坏，LTS 更相关

我们是否 100% 确定情况并非如此？不。

事实上，如果认为如此大规模的重写不会引入新的错误和/或意外的行为变化，那就太天真了。

> 对我来说，没有任何迹象表明他们在这里粗心。

有很多原因表明这一点包括缺乏 LTS 或任何在如此大规模的转变中投入的想法。

comments.author: atonse
comments.textZhHtml:
再说一遍，代码已经公开两个月了。而且据很多人说，很多人显然很乐意大声声讨所有出问题的地方。

对这次移植已经做了初步分析。而且代码还在继续重构。但是有没有哪篇铁证如山的文章，是有人真的发现了功能或稳定性上的回退？我们看到的情况恰恰相反。修复了几十个bug。我们不需要凭空推测。他们已经进行了两个月的实验，所有代码都公开透明。

只是感觉两个月过去了，人们还是想死死抱住这个_想法_，认为这太草率了，却拿不出任何造成重大负面影响的证据。

comments.author: srdjanr
comments.textZhHtml:
我对此有一些看法：

1）我（可能还有其他人）对此的最后印象是当它仅基于所有通过的测试进行合并时。在很多项目中，仅仅依靠测试肯定是鲁莽的（不确定Bun测试的覆盖率/质量）。

我没有太关注他们后来做了什么，也许确实足够好。例如，克劳德·科德（Claude Code）使用它并且表现良好令人放心。

2）快速做出如此重大的决定（甚至没有时间）咨询社区并不能真正激发信心。

3）即使最终一切都很完美，你也可能会鲁莽。

在城市中以 80 英里/小时的速度行驶，你可能有 80% 的机会不撞车。如果你没有撞车，你就会有一个更快、更有趣的旅程。并不意味着它不是鲁莽的。

comments.author: vincnetas
comments.textZhHtml:
这让我想起了魔兽世界经典：Leeroy Jenkins

yolo！！！

comments.author: jgalt212
comments.textZhHtml:
> 有一天，“别再闹了，我只是在玩这个”，几天后，“yolo 合并到 main 了”。

是的，这太荒谬了。

comments.author: padjo
comments.textZhHtml: 人们很快就依靠情感主张来攻击这一点，这很奇怪。就像我们是工程师一样，如果您能指出此重写的具体问题，我很乐意听到。显然，贾里德会给出积极的例子，他说他这样做并不能证明重写是一个坏主意。你需要指出客观问题，而不是你模糊的不安感。就目前而言，从所有可用的衡量标准来看，这似乎是一个巨大的成功，这绝对是非凡的。
